label: Heavy going
summary:
A Saturday defined by the aftershocks of a brutal week. The Lebanon ceasefire is in tatters — Hezbollah rejected it, Israel says it will not withdraw, and the humanitarian toll is staggering: more than 3,500 Lebanese killed and nearly a fifth of the population displaced since the war began in March. Iran's foreign minister warned that Israeli attacks on Lebanon violate the ceasefire 'on all fronts.' The UN warned the global hunger crisis is 'coming to pass' as the Hormuz closure nears 100 days, with WFP projecting 9 million could lose aid if the conflict lasts six months.

Markets closed Friday with heavy losses still echoing: the Nasdaq's 4% plunge wiped $1 trillion from semiconductors, and rising yields signalled the Fed may yet hike rather than cut. Oil dipped below $93 on weak Chinese demand but geopolitical risk keeps it elevated. Climate news is grim: NOAA forecasts above-normal heat across most of the US this month, and the ocean monitoring network continues to be dismantled. On a brighter note, the pancreatic cancer drug daraxonrasib doubled survival times in trials, platypuses are breeding on Australia's Hacking River for the first time in generations, and the World Cup pre-tournament friendlies offered a welcome distraction — Germany faced the US in Chicago.

drivers.headline: Lebanon ceasefire collapses: Hezbollah rejects deal, Israel refuses to withdraw; 3,500 Lebanese killed since March
Iran warns Israeli attacks in Lebanon violate ceasefire 'on all fronts'; no progress in US-Iran negotiations
